summary:
Molybdenum supports the enhancement of mechanical properties such as strength and corrosion/wear resistance without reducing them; it plays an essential supporting role rather than directly contributing through additive effects alone for all types considered here. The evidence does not support significant negative impacts from incorporating molybdenum into base metals like those found commonly used today across industries requiring robust material performance under varying conditions including high temperatures where traditional iron-based components might fail due to oxidation issues among others leading us towards better solutions when integrated properly within appropriate compositions designed around specific application needs thus making use beneficial albeit indirect but crucial one indeed! To summarize:

Molybdenum enhances the strength and durability of base metal alloys primarily via improvements in corrosion and wear resistance while having no detrimental effect on their inherent strengths. It serves more as a complementary element improving upon existing qualities instead of adding new ones independently. This aligns closely with understanding how minor adjustments can significantly bolster industrial applications relying heavily on reliable metallic structures capable enduring harsh environments
